record.field: Gitea is a lightweight, self-hosted all-in-one software development platform that brings GitHub-style Git hosting, code review, and CI/CD to your own infrastructure.

Gitea is a lightweight, self-hosted all-in-one software development platform that brings GitHub-style Git hosting, code review, and CI/CD to your own infrastructure.

Originally a community fork of Gogs, Gitea ships as a single Go binary and runs anywhere from a Raspberry Pi to a Kubernetes cluster. It bundles repository hosting, pull-request review, issue tracking, project boards, wikis, a built-in CI/CD engine and a multi-format package registry into one fast, low-footprint service. This deployment pairs Gitea with PostgreSQL as its database backend, the recommended setup for production and multi-team workloads.

Key features
  • Full Git hosting with pull requests, code review, branch protection and team permissions
  • Gitea Actions: integrated CI/CD compatible with the GitHub Actions YAML workflow format
  • Built-in package registry for 20+ formats (Container, npm, PyPI, Maven, NuGet, Helm and more)
  • Issue tracking, kanban project boards, wikis and team collaboration tools
  • PostgreSQL backend for reliable, scalable storage in production environments
  • Cross-platform single-binary design with minimal resource requirements
Best for

Teams and individuals who want a private, GitHub-like Git forge under their own control: development shops, DevOps teams running internal repositories and CI/CD, and self-hosters seeking a lightweight alternative to GitLab.

services: gitea: image: docker.gitea.com/gitea:1.24.4 restart: unless-stopped environment: - USER_UID=1000 - USER_GID=1000 - GITEA__database__DB_TYPE=postgres - GITEA__database__HOST=postgres:5432 - GITEA__database__NAME=gitea - GITEA__database__USER=gitea - GITEA__database__PASSWD=${GITEA_DB_PASSWORD:-gitea} volumes: - ./gitea_data:/data expose: - '3000' - '22' depends_on: - postgres healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -q - --spider - http://localhost:3000/ interval: 15s timeout: 5s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: docker.io/library/postgres:14 restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_USER=gitea - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${GITEA_DB_PASSWORD:-gitea} - POSTGRES_DB=gitea volumes: - ./pg_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data expose: - '5432' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
